Changes in domestic cereal production are approximately -50% in 2050 under the import dependency (ID) scenario relative to the Reference Future (RF).
Changes in domestic cereal consumption are approximately +9% in 2050 under the import dependency (ID) scenario relative to the Reference Future (RF).
Change in cereal production is -10.6% in 2050 under the climate change (CC) scenario relative to the Reference Future (RF).
Changes in cereal consumption are -9% in 2050 under the climate change (CC) scenario relative to the Reference Future (RF).
Change in cereal production is approximately -11.8% in 2050 under the import dependency (ID) scenario relative to the Reference Future (RF).
Changes in domestic cereal consumption are approximately +8.9% in 2050 under the import dependency (ID) scenario relative to the Reference Future (RF).
The increase in cereal consumption in the import dependency (ID) scenario is offset by the decrease in climate change (CC) scenario, resulting in a -1.8% change in the integrated case (IC) scenario.
